children.name: Panurus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing only the Bearded Reedling — a small, long-tailed bird of dense reedbeds. Despite the name, it is not a true tit (Paridae) but belongs to its own family. Its 'beard' is actually a striking black moustachial stripe on males. Highly social, they move through reeds in chattering flocks, clinging to vertical stems and acrobatically extracting seeds and insects.

children.name: Panurus biarmicus
children.description: A charismatic reedbed specialist found across Europe and Asia. Males have a blue-grey head, orange-buff body, and striking black moustachial stripes. Females are more uniformly warm brown. In Sweden it occurs in large reedbeds in the south and east, particularly at Lake Tåkern and Lake Hornborga. Its ringing 'ping' call is a characteristic sound of reedbeds in autumn.
